prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "平板支撑摸肩" (plank shoulder taps) exercise, front-three-quarter view. Person posture: Athletic person mid-rep of a plank shoulder tap: holding a high plank position (hands on the ground under shoulders with arms straight, toes on the ground, body in one straight line); currently lifting the right hand off the ground to tap the left shoulder while balancing the body on the left hand and both feet (the supporting hand is wider for stability); hips remain level and stable (no rotation); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: rectus abdominis and obliques (anti-rotation core work) and serratus anterior of the supporting arm. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: shoulder stabilizers and gluteus maximus and quadriceps. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹直肌 / 腹斜肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis and obliques (anti-rotation); "前锯肌" pointing to the serratus anterior of the supporting arm; "臀肌 / 肩稳定肌" pointing to the glute and shoulder stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
